docs.title: k8s-flagger-tester
docs.text: Flagger load-tester extended with hurl and k6 for HTTP assertion and load-test canary gates.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
k8s-flagger-tester extends the official Flagger load-tester with two additional tools pre-installed:

  • hurl — declarative HTTP testing with assertions, used as Flagger webhook gates to verify a canary is functionally correct before traffic is shifted
  • k6 — scriptable load testing, used as a Flagger webhook gate to verify latency and error-rate thresholds under synthetic load

All bundled tools are installed at their latest stable versions with explicit version ARGs, making upgrades a one-line diff.

docs.title: Why Debian?
docs.text: Alpine Linux uses musl libc. The hurl 8.x upstream releases a single Linux binary linked against glibc — there is no musl build. Running a glibc binary on Alpine requires a compat layer that is fragile and not officially supported. This image uses Debian bookworm-slim (glibc) as the runtime base. All static Go binaries (k6, kubectl, helm, ghz, grpc_health_probe) work unchanged on both.

docs.title: What's Included
docs.text: Tool Version Purpose flagger-loadtester 0.37.0 Canary webhook handler hurl 8.0.1 HTTP assertion testing k6 v2.0.0 Load testing helm v4.2.2 Chart operations kubectl v1.36.2 Cluster operations bats v1.13.0 Bash-based test suites ghz v0.121.0 gRPC benchmarking grpc_health_probe v0.4.52 gRPC health checks

docs.title: Canary Gate Flow
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant F as Flagger
    participant L as LoadTester
    participant H as hurl
    participant K as k6

    F->>L: pre-rollout webhook
    L->>H: hurl --test canary.hurl
    H-->>L: assertions pass
    L->>K: k6 run load-test.js
    K-->>L: p99 < threshold, error-rate < 1%
    L-->>F: 200 OK
    F->>F: promote canary

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: The changelog is auto-generated by release-please from conventional commits.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: All tool versions are declared as ARGs in the Dockerfile and can be overridden at build time.

docs.title: Build Arguments
docs.text: ARG Default Upstream HURL_VERSION 8.0.1 Orange-OpenSource/hurl K6_VERSION v2.0.0 grafana/k6 HELM_VERSION v4.2.2 helm/helm KUBECTL_VERSION v1.36.2 kubernetes/kubernetes GHZ_VERSION v0.121.0 bojand/ghz GRPC_HEALTH_PROBE_VERSION v0.4.52 grpc-ecosystem/grpc-health-probe

docs.title: Overriding Versions
docs.text:
docker build \
  --build-arg HURL_VERSION=8.1.0 \
  --build-arg K6_VERSION=v2.1.0 \
  -t my-flagger-tester:custom .

docs.title: Base Images
docs.text:
The Dockerfile uses three FROM stages, all tracked by Dependabot:

Stage Image Purpose loadtester ghcr.io/fluxcd/flagger-loadtester:0.37.0 Provides the webhook binary bats bats/bats:1.13.0 Provides the bats test runner final debian Runtime base (glibc)

Why Debian?

hurl releases a glibc-linked binary only. Debian (slim) is the cleanest base for running it without compatibility shims.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
mise run install

This installs Python dependencies and pre-commit hooks (including the commit-msg hook).

docs.title: Local Development
docs.text: Command What it does mise run lint Run all pre-commit hooks mise run docker-build-local Build image for local arch mise run docker-run Run the image on port 8080 mise run docker-preview Push ephemeral image to ttl.sh (6h TTL) mise run docs-serve Preview docs locally at http://localhost:8000

docs.title: Commit Style
docs.text:
Commits must follow Conventional Commits:

Type When to use feat New capability or tool added fix Bug fix chore Dependency bump, config change docs Documentation only ci CI/CD workflow change build Dockerfile change

The pre-commit commit-msg hook enforces this automatically.

docs.title: Pull Request Flow
docs.text:
1. Branch off main
  2. Make changes — pre-commit runs on every git commit
  3. Open a PR — CI runs lint, docker preview build, and Trivy security scan
  4. Merge — release-please creates a release PR with a version bump
  5. Release PR auto-merges — Docker image published to Docker Hub with latest + semver tags

docs.title: Dependency Updates
docs.text:
Two bots run side-by-side to keep every pinned version current:

Bot What it tracks Config Schedule Dependabot FROM base images · GitHub Actions SHAs .github/dependabot.yml Weekly Renovate ARG tool versions in Dockerfile renovate.json On push + scheduled

docs.title: Why Two Bots?
docs.text:
Dependabot understands Docker FROM lines and GitHub Actions SHAs natively — it cannot update ARG version strings inside a RUN layer.

Renovate fills that gap via regex-based custom managers, opening a PR whenever any tool version in the Dockerfile has a new upstream release.

docs.title: What Renovate Tracks
docs.text:
Each ARG in the Dockerfile is mapped to its upstream GitHub release source:

ARG Repository HURL_VERSION Orange-OpenSource/hurl K6_VERSION grafana/k6 HELM_VERSION helm/helm KUBECTL_VERSION kubernetes/kubernetes GHZ_VERSION bojand/ghz GRPC_HEALTH_PROBE_VERSION grpc-ecosystem/grpc-health-probe

docs.title: Release Cadence
docs.text: Every merged Dependabot or Renovate PR triggers a patch version bump via release-please, which auto-merges and publishes a new Docker image. The latest tag always points to the most recent release.

docs.title: FluxCD HelmRelease
docs.text:
Override the image in your Flagger load-tester HelmRelease values:

image:
  repository: vikas027/k8s-flagger-tester
  tag: latest

docs.title: Kubernetes Deployment
docs.text:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: flagger-loadtester
  namespace: test
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: loadtester
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: loadtester
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: loadtester
          image: vikas027/k8s-flagger-tester:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080

docs.title: Example Flagger Canary
docs.text:
apiVersion: flagger.app/v1beta1
kind: Canary
metadata:
  name: my-app
  namespace: default
spec:
  analysis:
    webhooks:
      - name: acceptance-test
        type: pre-rollout
        url: http://flagger-loadtester.test/
        timeout: 30s
        metadata:
          type: bash
          cmd: "hurl --test /tests/acceptance.hurl"
      - name: load-test
        url: http://flagger-loadtester.test/
        timeout: 5s
        metadata:
          type: bash
          cmd: "k6 run /tests/load.js"

docs.title: Pulling from Docker Hub
docs.text:
LatestPinned version 
docker pull vikas027/k8s-flagger-tester:latest

docker pull vikas027/k8s-flagger-tester:0.1.0
